claim_boundary: Ticket 62 tests whether TICKET61's pre-replay mod16 separator behaves like a lift-transition coordinate and transfers that discipline to RH, Goldbach, and Twin Prime. It does not prove or disprove any of the four open problems.

attempts.attempt: Transfer TICKET62's rule: after a pre-replay separator is found, the next proof obligation is a lift-stable transition law or an explicit collision witness, not another diagnostic separator.

bounded_result.transition_gate: pre-replay spectral separator must be closed under height-window lift
bounded_result.candidate_theorem: Every pre-replay zero-kernel separator state has a closed positivity transition law, or yields an off-critical collision packet.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a height lift whose zero-kernel positivity label collides inside the same symbolic separator state
bounded_result.frontier_rule: A separator can support proof only if the lift/height/cutoff transition is closed in the same coordinates or every failure is promoted to a named obstruction.

attempts.obstruction: This is a methodological transfer and does not solve the target problem. It raises the acceptance bar from finite separation to transition closure.
attempts.candidate_theorem: Every pre-replay zero-kernel separator state has a closed positivity transition law, or yields an off-critical collision packet.
attempts.next_experiment: Construct the problem-specific transition table or report the first collision witness.
attempts.claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.

attempts.attempt: Continue from TICKET61 by lifting the selected mixed 48-bit start-template rows to 52 and 56 bits, then testing whether the pre-replay mod16 separator remains deterministic under those lifts.

mod16_transition_cover_audit.discarded_route: Promote TICKET61's mod16 separator directly to an infinite theorem without checking higher-bit lift closure. TICKET62 treats that as an unproved shortcut.
mod16_transition_cover_audit.retained_route: Treat mod16 as a candidate state coordinate and test whether larger start-template lifts preserve deterministic failure-offset transitions. A collision becomes a theorem-obstruction witness; no collision becomes bounded evidence for a transition table, not a proof.
mod16_transition_cover_audit.candidate_theorem: For every mixed low40 cylinder and every admissible higher lift, the low40 plus high-extension mod16 state either determines a closed failure-offset transition or enters a finite automaton cover with no full-period nondecreasing cycle.
mod16_transition_cover_audit.counterexample_target: A higher start-template lift where low40 plus base mod16 admits two different failure offsets, or a full-period replay inside the tested lift family.
mod16_transition_cover_audit.proof_boundary: This is a bounded lift-transition audit over selected mixed cylinders. It can refute a naive mod16 transition theorem or name the next automaton cover target, but it does not prove Collatz and does not certify a Collatz counterexample.

attempts.obstruction: Even when a finite lift audit survives, it is not an infinite transition theorem. If a collision appears, it refutes only the naive mod16 theorem and points to a new coordinate; it is not a Collatz counterexample unless it becomes a full-period nondecreasing orbit.
attempts.candidate_theorem: For every mixed low40 cylinder and every admissible higher lift, the low40 plus high-extension mod16 state either determines a closed failure-offset transition or enters a finite automaton cover with no full-period nondecreasing cycle.
attempts.next_experiment: Generate CO-TICKET-63 by either proving the retained transition table symbolically or adding the smallest new coordinate required by the first lift collision.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Collatz proof and no certified Collatz counterexample.

bounded_result.transition_gate: pre-replay margin separator must be closed under cutoff lift
bounded_result.candidate_theorem: Every Goldbach margin separator state has a closed cutoff transition law, or yields a stable exceptional residue packet.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a cutoff lift whose positive-margin label collides inside the same character/error separator state
bounded_result.frontier_rule: A separator can support proof only if the lift/height/cutoff transition is closed in the same coordinates or every failure is promoted to a named obstruction.

attempts.obstruction: This is a methodological transfer and does not solve the target problem. It raises the acceptance bar from finite separation to transition closure.

bounded_result.candidate_theorem: Every twin-prime sieve separator state has a closed retained-mass transition law, or yields a stable exact-gap mass leak.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a sieve-level lift whose exact-gap label collides inside the same parity/error separator state
bounded_result.frontier_rule: A separator can support proof only if the lift/height/cutoff transition is closed in the same coordinates or every failure is promoted to a named obstruction.
